sanskrit:
न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते।
त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः॥
transliteration:
na dveṣṭyakuśalaṃ karma kuśale nānuṣajjate |
tyāgī sattvasamāviṣṭo medhāvī chinnasaṃśayaḥ ||
literal_meaning: na—never; dveṣṭi—hates; akuśalam—inauspicious; karma—work; kuśale—in auspicious; na—nor; anuṣajjate—becomes attached; tyāgī—the renouncer; sattva—goodness; samāviṣṭaḥ—absorbed in; medhāvī—intelligent; chinna—cut up; saṃśayaḥ—all doubts.
contextual_meaning: A psychologically integrated person neither recoils from difficult tasks nor clings to pleasant ones; free of attraction-aversion, they act with clarity and without inner conflict.
psychological_state: Flow-state: actions are decoupled from egoic reward, producing effortless engagement and zero rumination.
trigger_scenario: You’re asked to lead a high-stakes project at work that nobody wants; you accept without grumbling, delegate without micromanaging, and leave the outcome out of your self-worth.
anchor_text: Work calmly, neither chasing what feels good nor fleeing what feels hard.
